text.title: Technical Debt Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Technical Debt Management. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: All known debt is captured in a register; invisible debt is unmanaged debt.
rule.rationale: All known debt is captured in a register; invisible debt is unmanaged debt.

rule.statement: 10-20% of sprint capacity allocated to debt work; non-negotiable.
rule.rationale: 10-20% of sprint capacity allocated to debt work; non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Each item carries interest rate = cost-of-living-with × likelihood-of-incident.
rule.rationale: Each item carries interest rate = cost-of-living-with × likelihood-of-incident.

rule.statement: Within budget, top-interest items go first.
rule.rationale: Within budget, top-interest items go first.

rule.statement: Zero-debt sprints are forbidden; the next one always becomes crisis.
rule.rationale: Zero-debt sprints are forbidden; the next one always becomes crisis.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-technical-debt-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
